subject: a getaway driver's last job
lyrics:
[Verse]
Keys in my teeth, engine coughing black
Three minutes on the clock, no looking back
They said one more run and I'm finally clean
Told that lie to every windshield I've seen

[Chorus]
FOOT TO THE FLOOR — this is the last one
FOOT TO THE FLOOR — before the sun
I memorized the alley, the curb, the wall
God help the brakes if I have to stall

[Verse]
They come out running, bags ripping at the seam
Siren in the mirror chewing up my dream
Fourth gear screaming, redline in the red
A bullet wrote its name above my head

[Bridge]
My daughter's car seat still bolted in the back
That's the only reason I want the road, not the track
If I make this corner I swear I quit for good
Liars say that too, deep in the neighborhood

[Chorus]
FOOT TO THE FLOOR — this is the last one
FOOT TO THE FLOOR — before the sun
Wheels off the bridge or wheels on the run
Either way the engine and the job are done

subject: a barber who knows the whole town
lyrics:
[Verse]
Neon hums pink on the chrome of his chair
Scissors keep time like a heartbeat in there
Every jaw in this city has sat where you sit
He holds all the secrets a straight razor split

[Chorus]
He knows who's leaving, he knows who's in love
He knows whose father is watching from above
Under the buzzing magenta fluorescent light
The whole town confesses on Saturday night

[Verse]
The mayor's grey roots, the widower's beard
The boy's first fade and the things that he feared
A mirror so old it remembers their faces
Reflecting back twenty years of small places

[Bridge]
When the strip mall goes dark and the cassette runs out
He sweeps up the gossip nobody talks about
Locks the glass door on a town in his head
Full of every word that was cut and not said

[Chorus]
He knows who's leaving, he knows who's in love
He knows whose father is watching from above
Under the buzzing magenta fluorescent light
The whole town confesses on Saturday night

subject: a summer fling at the beach
lyrics:
[Verse]
Sand in the speaker, half a warm soda
You drew our initials, the wave came and stole 'em
Sunscreen and salt on the back of your neck
A Polaroid fading before I could check

[Chorus]
Just for a season, no promises made
Melting like cones in the boardwalk shade
We knew it would end with the cooling of June
So we kissed like the tide wasn't coming back soon

[Verse]
Flip-flop tan lines, a bonfire low
You packing the trunk in the morning, gotta go
I keep one shell that still smells like the bay
Humming a beat that I made of that day

[Bridge]
No number, no last name, just a laugh and a glance
A two-week forever, a temporary dance
Winter can have me, I'll trade it the heat
For a girl and a chorus that loops on repeat

[Chorus]
Just for a season, no promises made
Melting like cones in the boardwalk shade
We knew it would end with the cooling of June
So we kissed like the tide wasn't coming back soon

subject: an elder teaching the tide
lyrics:
[Verse]
Grandfather stands where the reef meets the foam
Salt in his dreadlocks, the ocean his home
He say, watch how the water remember the moon
It leave and it come, never early, never soon

[Chorus]
Learn the tide, learn the tide, little one
It rise when it rise, it run when it run
You cannot push it, you cannot hold it tight
Just paddle with the morning and rest with the night

[Verse]
He read the green water like print on a page
Where the snapper run, where the current grow its rage
Hands rough as coral, eyes soft as the spray
Fifty years of listening to what the sea say

[Bridge]
One day the boy stood alone on the sand
Felt the pull of the water, the wisdom in his hand
Heard the old man's voice in the breaking of the wave
The tide is the teacher and patience is the brave

[Chorus]
Learn the tide, learn the tide, little one
It rise when it rise, it run when it run
You cannot push it, you cannot hold it tight
Just paddle with the morning and rest with the night

subject: a card game that went too far
lyrics:
[Verse]
Four men, one bulb, a felt gone soft with years
The deck snapped clean, the room thick with arrears
Nobody folded when the smart thing was to fold
Pride doubled down on a hand it couldn't hold

[Chorus]
The pot kept rising past the money on the wood
Past the cars, the deeds, the things a man called good
We pushed it all in where the river runs to debt
And nobody at the table has walked away yet

[Verse]
A watch from a father slid into the pile
The wedding ring followed without a smile
The clock on the wall stopped meaning a thing
When a friendship got wagered on a king

[Bridge]
Slow build — the cards turn over one by one
the lamp swings — the silence weighs a ton
the loser stands — the chair scrapes like a bell
the winner can't look at the story he'll have to tell

[Chorus]
The pot kept rising past the money on the wood
Past the cars, the deeds, the things a man called good
We pushed it all in where the river runs to debt
And nobody at the table has walked away yet

subject: teaching your daughter to drive
lyrics:
[Verse]
Empty lot at dawn, frost still on the glass
Your hands at ten and two, afraid to let it pass
The engine like a heartbeat under both our feet
I press a phantom brake on my side of the seat

[Chorus]
Go easy on the gas, the whole world's up ahead
I'm letting go the wheel I held instead
A blur of streetlights washing pale and wide
My little girl is learning how to drive

[Verse]
You laughed when the cones went scattering away
I swallowed every warning I was dying to say
The radio a hush beneath the morning haze
I'm memorizing this, the last of the slow days

[Bridge]
One hand on the door and one hand on the past
The mirror full of someone growing up too fast
Soon you'll take the highway where I cannot ride
So turn the key, my darling, and learn to leave my side

[Chorus]
Go easy on the gas, the whole world's up ahead
I'm letting go the wheel I held instead
A blur of streetlights washing pale and wide
My little girl is learning how to drive

subject: the northern lights
lyrics:
[Verse]
Dead-end town, no money, no plan
Gas tank empty in a rusted-out van
Then the sky cracked open green over the pines
Nobody asked permission, nobody signed

[Chorus]
LOOK UP — the heavens are rioting tonight
Green and violet smashing every light
No cop, no boss, no border up there
Just the sky throwing color like it doesn't care

[Verse]
We climbed the water tower, freezing and broke
Watched the universe lighting its smoke
The whole rotten system couldn't bill us a dime
For the wildest free show happening one time

[Bridge]
They can't fence it, they can't sell it, they can't tax the glow
The one anarchist painting the cold sky throws
So scream at the colors till your throat goes raw
The aurora don't answer to anybody's law

[Chorus]
LOOK UP — the heavens are rioting tonight
Green and violet smashing every light
No cop, no boss, no border up there
Just the sky throwing color like it doesn't care

subject: working two jobs and still falling behind
lyrics:
[Verse]
Clock in at the warehouse, clock out, clock in again
Apron at the diner by a quarter past ten
Two name tags, one body, no time to break
Every dollar I catch is a dollar they take

[Chorus]
Running, running, but the ground keep moving
Bust my back all week and I got nothing to show for it
Rent went up, the check stayed flat
Working like a slave and they call me lazy for that

[Verse]
Gas light blinking on the way to shift two
Noodles in the microwave, that's dinner for two
Daughter doing homework while I count out the tips
Kiss her on the forehead with exhaustion on my lips

[Bridge]
They say pull yourself up — I been pulling so hard
The rope's just a debt that I drag through the yard
No days off, no sick pay, no cushion underneath
Just a hustle and a prayer and a grinding of teeth

[Chorus]
Running, running, but the ground keep moving
Bust my back all week and I got nothing to show for it
Rent went up, the check stayed flat
Working like a slave and they call me lazy for that

subject: a New Orleans funeral
lyrics:
[Verse]
Slow march out of Tremé, the tuba breathing low
Black umbrellas tipping in a sad and steady row
A hymn drag heavy down the cracked banquette
We carry brother home through the heat and the sweat

[Chorus]
Don't you cry forever, let the second line roll
The body's in the ground but the music's got the soul
From a dirge to a dance when we turn the corner round
New Orleans say goodbye with a joyful sound

[Verse]
Then the snare hit double and the trumpet caught fire
Handkerchiefs waving, lifting higher and higher
Grandmama spinning where the tears used to be
The street become a river setting sorrow free

[Bridge]
We known you in the church and we known you in the bar
Now we strut you to the sky on a melody and a star
So cut the brass loose, let the bass drum pound
Death ain't the ending in this sacred ground

[Chorus]
Don't you cry forever, let the second line roll
The body's in the ground but the music's got the soul
From a dirge to a dance when we turn the corner round
New Orleans say goodbye with a joyful sound

subject: a grandmother's hands
lyrics:
[Verse]
Flour on her knuckles, a thimble worn thin
Maps of every winter pressed into her skin
They rocked my mother, they buried two sons
They held the whole family when the hard times come

[Chorus]
Those hands, those hands, brown as the riverbed
They braided my hair and they buttered my bread
Never asked for nothing, gave away the rest
Lord, those hands were the kindest I was blessed

[Verse]
They snapped the green beans on a porch in July
Wiped my fevered forehead, taught me how to fly
Clasped at the table when she bowed to pray
Shaking just a little more with every passing day

[Bridge]
Now they rest so quiet on the quilt that she sewed
Still warm when I hold them at the end of the road
Everything I am, every word that I write
Was folded in those palms on a Sunday night

[Chorus]
Those hands, those hands, brown as the riverbed
They braided my hair and they buttered my bread
Never asked for nothing, gave away the rest
Lord, those hands were the kindest I was blessed

subject: the last night of a festival
lyrics:
[Verse]
The Ferris wheel's slowing, the lights coming down
Last band on the stage in a sleepy little town
Paper cups rolling where the dancers used to sway
Hold me close, baby, before they pack away

[Chorus]
One more night, sha-la-la, one more song
The summer's almost over but it lasted all night long
Don't let go, my darling, while the lanterns still glow
It's the last of the festival, oh-oh, the last show

[Verse]
The cotton candy stand is folding up its shade
A boy buys a rose with the last dime he made
The stars hang low like they don't want to leave
Kiss me by the fountain on this August eve

[Bridge]
Woah-oh, the band plays slow
Woah-oh, the crowd lets go
Morning's gonna take this away
So love me, love me, love me till the gray

[Chorus]
One more night, sha-la-la, one more song
The summer's almost over but it lasted all night long
Don't let go, my darling, while the lanterns still glow
It's the last of the festival, oh-oh, the last show

subject: the one that got away
lyrics:
[Verse]
There's a road outside Galway I never drove down
A girl with a fiddle and a green velvet gown
I was young and I was certain there'd be time to come back
Now the years pile like snow on a long-buried track

[Chorus]
Oh, the one that got away, she's a song I can't end
A verse with no chorus, a break that won't mend
I married, I prospered, I built a good life
But the wind in the chimney still whispers her name at night

[Verse]
We parted at a station with the rain coming hard
I said I would write her, I never sent the card
Pride is a country with a cold, locked door
And I've stood on the wrong side of it ever more

[Bridge]
They say she lives east now with children and a man
I hope that he's gentle, I hope that he can
Give her what I couldn't when I was a fool
With my whole life ahead and my heart still in school

[Chorus]
Oh, the one that got away, she's a song I can't end
A verse with no chorus, a break that won't mend
I married, I prospered, I built a good life
But the wind in the chimney still whispers her name at night

subject: an elder teaching the tide
lyrics:
[Verse]
Pick up the offbeat, the old man's on the pier
Straw hat tilting, calling, come over here
He taps out the rhythm of the in and the out
That's the tide, little soldier, that's what it's about

[Chorus]
Up on the upstroke, down with the moon
The sea got a horn section playing in tune
Learn it, learn it, skank with the swell
The old man's teaching what the ocean can't tell

[Verse]
He count the waves like a trumpet count bars
Reef on the downbeat, low tide pulling stars
Quick feet, quick feet, the water never late
Dance with the current, don't try to be its weight

[Bridge]
(Pick it up!)
The horns hit, the foam fly, the kid finally see
The whole bouncing rhythm running wild and free
The elder just grin with a gap-tooth pride
Welcome to the band, son — welcome to the tide

[Chorus]
Up on the upstroke, down with the moon
The sea got a horn section playing in tune
Learn it, learn it, skank with the swell
The old man's teaching what the ocean can't tell

subject: the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
lyrics:
[Verse]
The door swings open and the years fall away
Garlic and oregano, a wood stove's gray
A pot that has simmered since before I was born
Breathing the steam of a thousand worn morns

[Chorus]
Ah, that kitchen, that kitchen, it lives in my chest
The smell of her cooking is the home I love best
Close my eyes in the city, in the cold and the noise
And I'm five years old in her saffron-warm joys

[Verse]
Flour like a snowfall across her dark dress
The knife and the cutting board, the beautiful mess
Wine in the sauce and a song on her tongue
The whole house alive with the breakfasts she'd hung

[Bridge]
Now the stove has gone cold and the recipe's mine
I measure with memory, I cook by her sign
And just for a moment when the onions turn sweet
She's standing behind me, her ghost at my feet

[Chorus]
Ah, that kitchen, that kitchen, it lives in my chest
The smell of her cooking is the home I love best
Close my eyes in the city, in the cold and the noise
And I'm five years old in her saffron-warm joys

subject: looking back at your first open mic
lyrics:
[Verse]
A stool and a spotlight, my hands made of stone
Six strings out of tune and a microphone's drone
The coffee machine hissing right through my song
I forgot the second verse, I played the bridge wrong

[Chorus]
But oh, how I shimmered in that little café glow
Green as the spring and too foolish to know
That the trembling and stumbling were the sweetest of all
The softest, the warmest, the first gentle fall

[Verse]
Four people clapping and three of them mine
A waitress half-listening, a candle, some wine
My voice like a sparrow that lands then takes flight
The bravest small thing I have done in my life

[Bridge]
Now I've played the big rooms with the velvet and gold
But none of them shimmer like that night of old
Where nobody knew me and I sang anyway
Into the warm hush of an ordinary day

[Chorus]
But oh, how I shimmered in that little café glow
Green as the spring and too foolish to know
That the trembling and stumbling were the sweetest of all
The softest, the warmest, the first gentle fall
